volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Self-Tormentor (Section 2.3.380d - Section 3.1.469b)

sections.label: Section 2.3.380d
sections.translation: Take yourself off .

sections.label: Section 2.4.381
sections.original: Edepol te , mea Antiphila , laudo et fortunatam iudico ,
sections.translation: Upon my word , my dear Antiphila , I commend you , and think you fortunate in having made it your study that your manners should be conformable to those good looks of yours : and so may the Gods bless me , I do not at all wonder if every man is in love with you . For your discourse has been a proof to me what kind of disposition you possess .

sections.label: Section 2.4.385
sections.original: Et quum egomet nunc mecum in animo vitam tuam considero ,
sections.translation: And when now I reflect in my mind upon your way of life , and that of all of you , in fact , who keep the public at a distance from yourselves , it is not surprising both that you are of that disposition , and that we are not ; for it is your interest to be virtuous ; those , with whom we are acquainted , will not allow us to be so . For our lovers , allured merely by our beauty , court us for that ;

sections.label: Section 2.4.390
sections.original: Haec ubi immutata est , illi suum animum alio conferunt .
sections.translation: when that has faded , they transfer their affections elsewhere ; and unless we have made provision in the mean time for the future , we live in destitution . Now with you , when you have once resolved to pass your life with one man whose manners are especially kindred to your own , those persons [*] become attached to you . By this kindly feeling , you are truly devoted to each other ;

sections.label: Section 2.4.395
sections.original: Ut numquam ulla amori vestro incidere possit calamitas .
sections.translation: and no calamity can ever possibly interrupt your love .

sections.label: Section 2.4.396
sections.original: Nescio alias : me quidem semper scio fecisse sedulo
sections.translation: I know nothing about other women : I ’ m sure that I have , indeed , always used every endeavor to derive my own happiness from his happiness .

sections.label: Section 2.4.397b
sections.translation: Ah ! ’ tis for that reason , my Antiphila , that you alone have now caused me to return to my native country ; for while I was absent from you , all other hardships which I encountered were light to me ,

sections.label: Section 2.4.400
sections.translation: save the being deprived of you .

sections.label: Section 2.4.400b
sections.translation: I believe it .

sections.label: Section 2.4.400c
sections.translation: Syrus , I can scarce endure it ! [*] Wretch that I am , that I should not be allowed to possess one of such a disposition at my own discretion !

sections.label: Section 2.4.402
sections.original: Immo , ut patrem tuum vidi esse habitum , diu etiam duras dabit .
sections.translation: Nay , so far as I understand your father , he will for a long time yet be giving you a hard task .

sections.label: Section 2.4.403
sections.original: Quisnam hic adolescens est qui intuitur nos ?
sections.translation: Why , who is that young man that ’ s looking at us ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.403b
sections.translation: Ah ! do support me , I entreat you !

sections.label: Section 2.4.404
sections.translation: Prithee , what is the matter with you ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.404b
sections.translation: I shall die , alas ! I shall die !

sections.label: Section 2.4.404c
sections.translation: Why are you thus surprised , Antiphila ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.405b
sections.translation: Is it Clinia that I see , or not ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.405c
sections.translation: Whom do you see ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.406
sections.translation: Blessings on you , my life !

sections.label: Section 2.4.406b
sections.translation: Oh my long-wished for Clinia , blessings on you !

sections.label: Section 2.4.406c
sections.translation: How fare you , my love ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.407
sections.translation: I ’ m overjoyed that you have returned safe .

sections.label: Section 2.4.407b
sections.translation: And do I embrace you , Antiphila , so passionately longed for by my soul ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.409
sections.original: Ite intro : nam vos iamdudum exspectat senex .
sections.translation: Go in-doors ; for the old gentleman has been waiting for us some time .

sections.label: Section 3.1.410
sections.original: Luciscit hoc iam : cesso pultare ostium
sections.translation: It is now daybreak . [*] Why do I delay to knock at my neighbor ’ s door , that he may learn from me the first that his son has returned ? Although I am aware that the youth would not prefer this . But when I see him tormenting himself so miserably about his absence , can I conceal a joy so unhoped for ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.415
sections.original: Cum illi pericli nihil ex indicio siet ?
sections.translation: especially when there can be no danger to him from the discovery ? I will not do so ; but as far as I can I will assist the old man . As I see my son aiding his friend and year ’ s-mate , and acting as his confidant in his concerns , it is but right that we old men as well should assist each other .

sections.label: Section 3.1.420
sections.original: Aut ego profecto ingenio egregie ad miserias
sections.translation: Assuredly I was either born with a disposition peculiarly suited for misery , or else that saying which I hear commonly repeated , that time assuages human sorrow , is false . For really my sorrow about my son increases daily ; and the longer

sections.label: Section 3.1.425
sections.original: Abest , magis cupio tanto et magis desidero .
sections.translation: he is away from me , the more anxiously do I wish for him , and the more I miss him .

sections.label: Section 3.1.426
sections.original: Sed ipsum foras egressum video : ibo , alloquar .
sections.translation: But I see him coming out of his house ; I ’ ll go speak to him . ( Aloud .) Menedemus , good-morrow ; I bring you news , which you would especially desire to be imparted .

sections.label: Section 3.1.429
sections.original: Numquidnam de gnato meo audisti , Chreme ?
sections.translation: Pray , have you heard any thing about my son , Chremes ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.430
sections.translation: He ’ s alive , and well .

sections.label: Section 3.1.430b
sections.translation: Why , where is he , pray ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.430c
sections.translation: Here , at my house , at home .

sections.label: Section 3.1.431
sections.translation: My son ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.431b
sections.translation: Such is the fact .

sections.label: Section 3.1.431c
sections.translation: Come home ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.431d
sections.translation: Certainly .

sections.label: Section 3.1.431e
sections.translation: My son , Clinia , come home ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.432b
sections.translation: I say so .

sections.label: Section 3.1.432c
sections.original: Eamus : duc me ad eum , obsecro .
sections.translation: Let us go . Lead me to him , I beg of you .

sections.label: Section 3.1.433
sections.original: Non vult te scire se redisse etiam , et tuum
sections.translation: He does not wish you yet to know of his return , and he shuns your presence ; he ’ s afraid that ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.435
sections.original: Ne tua duritia antiqua illa etiam adaucta sit .
sections.translation: on account of that fault , your former severity may even be increased .

sections.label: Section 3.1.436
sections.original: Non tu ei dixisti ut essem ?
sections.translation: Did you not tell him how I was affected ? [*]

sections.label: Section 3.1.436b
sections.translation: No —

sections.label: Section 3.1.436c
sections.translation: For what reason , Chremes ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.437
sections.original: Quia pessime istoc in te atque in illum consulis ,
sections.translation: Because there you would judge extremely ill both for yourself and for him , if you were to show yourself of a spirit so weak and irresolute .

sections.label: Section 3.1.439
sections.original: Non possum : satis iam satis pater durus fui .
sections.translation: I can not help it : enough already , enough , have I proved a rigorous father .

sections.label: Section 3.1.439b
sections.translation: Ah

sections.label: Section 3.1.440
sections.original: Vehemens in utramque partem , Menedeme , es nimis ,
sections.translation: Menedemus ! you are too precipitate in either extreme , either with profuseness or with parsimony too great . Into the same error will you fall from the one side as from the other . In the first place , formerly , rather than allow your son to visit a young woman , who was

sections.label: Section 3.1.445
sections.original: Tum erat contenta , cuique erant grata omnia ,
sections.translation: then content with a very little , and to whom any thing was acceptable , you frightened him away from here . After that , she began , quite against her inclination , to seek a subsistence upon the town . Now , when she can not be supported without a great expense , you are ready to give any thing . For , that you may know

sections.label: Section 3.1.450
sections.original: Quam ea nunc instructa pulchre ad perniciem siet ,
sections.translation: how perfectly she is trained to extravagance , in the first place , she has already brought with her more than ten female attendants , all laden with clothes and jewels of gold ; if a satrap [*] had been her admirer , he never could support her expenses , much less can you .

sections.label: Section 3.1.454b
sections.translation: Is she at your house ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.454c
sections.translation: Is she , do you ask ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.455
sections.original: Sensi : namque ei unam coenam atque eius comitibus
sections.translation: I have felt it ; for I have given her and her retinue one dinner ; had I to give them another such , it would be all over with me ; for , to pass by other matters , what a quantity of wine she did consume for me in tasting only , [*] saying thus , This wine is too acid , [*] respected sir , [*] do please look for something more mellow .

sections.label: Section 3.1.460
sections.translation: I opened all the casks , all the vessels [*] ; she kept all on the stir : and this but a single night . What do you suppose will become of you when they are constantly preying upon you ? So may the Gods prosper me , Menedemus , I do pity your lot .

sections.label: Section 3.1.464b
sections.translation: Let him do what he will ;

sections.label: Section 3.1.465
sections.original: Sumat , consumat , perdat , decretum est pati ,
sections.translation: let him take , waste , and squander ; I ’ m determined to endure it , so long as I only have him with me .

sections.label: Section 3.1.466b
sections.translation: If it is your determination thus to act , I hold it to be of very great foment that he should not be aware that with a full knowledge you grant him this .

sections.label: Section 3.1.469
sections.translation: What shall I do ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.469b
sections.translation: Any thing , rather than what you are thinking of ;
